MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Scott Righthand (Scott@righthandlaw.net) for Plaintiff.
Janell M. Alberto (JAlberto@wshblaw.com) of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendants/Moving Parties.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs DON SMITH, MADISON MRH-1 FRANKLIN, LLC, TOUCHSTONE GOLF, LLC's MOTION TO TRANSFER:

Defendants' motion to change venue is granted. Moving parties show, and the Court finds, that the sole defendant resident in San Francisco, Franklin Canyon Corporation, was "improperly joined" or "has been made a defendant solely for the purpose of having the action tried" in San Francisco. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 395(a).) Franklin Canyon Corporation is not the owner of the golf course where the underlying accident occurred and, besides the coincidence of its name, has no connection whatever with the underlying accident, which occurred in Contra Costa County. (Heringer v. Schumacher (1928) 88 Cal.App. 349, 351 ["the law is well settled that, upon a motion to change the place of trial, where the question as to whether a defendant has been joined improperly for the purpose of having the cause tried in a particular county, the court has a right to look behind the complaint, and see if any defendant has been improperly joined for the purpose of retaining the action in the county where the suit is begun."].) An action for personal injury or death is triable either in the county where defendants (or one of them) reside or in the county where the injury occurred (including the injury causing the death). (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 395(a).) The Court orders the case transferred to Contra Costa County, where the injury occurred.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
